title: taraxacum farinosum
intro: Taraxacum farinosum, also known as the sticky dandelion or sticky meadow dandelion, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family that is characterized by its hairy, sticky leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Taraxacum farinosum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Taraxacum farinosum is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
